prompt: A manufacturing company reported a revenue of $500,000 in Quarter 1 (Q1). In Quarter 2 (Q2), its revenue increased by 15% compared to Q1. Operating costs are consistently 60% of the total revenue for any quarter. For Quarter 3 (Q3), the company launched a new product line which resulted in a 20% increase in the number of units sold compared to Q2, but also led to a 5% decrease in the average price per unit compared to Q2. Operating costs in Q3 remained at 60% of the Q3 revenue. What was the company's net profit in Quarter 3?
